Container.Name: statesync
Container.Overview: Keeps Emby and Jellyfin watch states in sync.
Container.Description:
StateSync connects to your Emby and Jellyfin servers via their HTTP and WebSocket APIs. When a user pauses, resumes, or finishes a show on one server, the change is pushed to the other within a few seconds. Supports any number of Emby and Jellyfin servers (the daemon discovers new users automatically).
Open the web UI on port 4407 after install to add servers. API keys are stored in the config volume and masked in the UI.
Standard Unraid PUID/PGID/UMASK variables are honored. Defaults (99/100/022) match the 'nobody' user used by other community apps (binhex-syncthing, glances, ollama, etc.) so the appdata dir shows as 'nobody' in the Unraid file manager. Change PUID/PGID to run as a different user if you prefer.
Use the REFRESH and FORCE SYNC buttons in the dashboard (top of the MAPPED USERS section) to manually reload the user list or push all historical played state across servers.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port the dashboard listens on. Open this in your browser after the container starts.

Config.Name: Config Volume
Config.Description: Folder on the array where config.json is stored. Persists across container updates. Owned by PUID:PGID (default nobody:users).

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Your local timezone, e.g. America/New_York or Europe/Berlin. Used only for log timestamps.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for the running container. Default 99 (Unraid's 'nobody'). Files created by the daemon will be owned by this uid.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for the running container. Default 100 (Unraid's 'users' group).

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: UMASK for the running container. Default 022. Use 000 for group-writable configs (matches binhex-syncthing's permissive perms).

Config.Name: Log Verbosity
Config.Description: How chatty the logs are. info is normal. Use debug for troubleshooting (very chatty).

Config.Name: Plain HTTP to Emby/Jellyfin
Config.Description: Whether StateSync is allowed to talk to your Emby/Jellyfin servers over plain HTTP (no TLS). Default 'true' because most homelab servers use HTTP. Set 'false' to require HTTPS — only needed if your media servers are exposed beyond the LAN (e.g. behind a reverse proxy with TLS) and you don't want the API key crossing an unencrypted link.

Config.Name: Dashboard Password
Config.Description: OPTIONAL. Leave blank to let anyone on your LAN use the dashboard. To require a password, paste a token in the format: bearer:YOURTOKEN (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32)
